Settings saved

Playback

300 WPM
Adaptive chunk size
Let the reader go a little shorter or longer than your chosen chunk size when the text calls for it.
Tune pacing and visual emphasis for different kinds of writing.
Read aloud
Speak the exact chunk currently on screen and wait for that chunk to finish before advancing.
Chrome TTS stays as the default and fallback.
KittenTTS Nano
A lightweight CPU model downloaded only if you opt in.
Not downloaded
~24 MB Apache-2.0 Runs locally after download
0%
Local neural TTS is optional. If it is unavailable, read aloud falls back to Chrome TTS.
Used by Chrome TTS and as the backup voice.
1.0×
Used when adaptive chunk size is on.
Warmup ramp
Start slow, ramp up to full speed over the first 15 chunks
Adaptive pacing
Slower for punctuation, numbers, long words; faster for stop words
Sentence mode
Snap chunk boundaries to sentence ends for better comprehension
Auto-advance chapters
After the chapter ends, continue after a 3 second countdown you can cancel
1.8×
Customize per punctuation type
Clause , ; :
1.3×
Sentence end . ! ?
1.8×
Dash — –
2.0×
Ellipsis … ...
2.0×

Training

Speed trainer
Turn on lightweight streaks and session goals without changing the core reader.
Level 1
Points 0
Streak 0 days
Sessions 0
Best WPM 0
Best Focus Run 0 words
Active Time 0:00
Challenges Cleared 0
Next challenge
This is the goal the reader will track live while you read.
+0 pts
Distance Builder
Read a few hundred words in one focused sitting.
Last qualifying session
No qualifying sessions yet. Read for at least a short focused stretch to start your streak.

Display

48px
97%
Reading
Peripheral vision mode
Keep the previous and next chunk faintly visible to reduce hard flashes.

ADHD features

Bionic reading
Bold the first 45% of each word's letters to anchor the eye
Bionic reading makes words easier to scan
Focal point (ORP guide)
A fixed guide line at the Optimal Recognition Point — train your eye to stay still
Show session stats
Display live reading stats below the reader controls
Highlight current paragraph
Keep the source page in sync with the active chunk

Supported pages

Choose which parsers are active, and star favorites so they appear as quick links in the popup.

Follow translated page text
If Brave or Chrome Translate rewrites the current page, keep your word position and refresh the reader from the translated DOM when possible.
Parsers

Non-English scripts are supported as long as the page exposes readable text in the DOM.

Translated pages work best when the browser replaces the visible page text directly.

WTR-LAB defaults to the webplus view by appending ?service=webplus.

Keyboard shortcuts

Click a shortcut to rebind it, then press the new key.

Global shortcut: Alt+R — open/close reader from any page

Personalization

Enable personalized pacing
Opt in to a fully local pacing model trained on rewinds, pauses, and speed changes.
Consent to local training data
Required before the extension can log reading events for the on-device model.
Training status

Words since last training: 0

Last trained: Never

Events stored: 0

Comfort trace
No personalization data yet.

Bookmarks

No bookmarks yet. They're saved automatically when you close the reader.

Feedback

Show feedback widget in reader
Display a small feedback area below the reader to quickly submit thoughts.
Send us your thoughts

Feedback is completely anonymous. We do not collect any personal information.

Advanced

Enable cloud syncing
Securely sync your settings across devices using Chrome's built-in storage sync.
Check for updates
Occasionally checks GitHub to see if a newer version of RSVP Reader is available.
Export / import bundle
Includes settings, bookmarks, and personalization data in a single .rsvp file.
About

RSVP Reader v0.1.0

100% local — no accounts, no tracking, no servers.

Storage used: …
